title: Cold Chain Intake
body: Field tissue vials stay on ice after pickup. Staff scan the cooler tag every fifteen minutes and move the vials to the blue freezer before the two hour transfer window ends. The cold chain note is about sample custody, not alarm repair.

title: Label Reconciliation
body: Accession labels are checked against the intake sheet before storage. A vial with a mismatched barcode is held in the review tray until two staff members reconcile the label and sign the correction log.

title: Chain of Custody Audit
body: The custody audit compares cooler scan times, storage arrival, and signed label corrections after intake is complete. It lists barcode disagreement counts for trend review, but it does not hold or fix a live vial before storage.

title: Reagent Quarantine
body: Expired reagent lots are sealed in amber bins and marked unavailable. The quarantine log records lot number, expiration date, and shelf location so technicians do not use reagents that only look like active stock.

title: Freezer Alarm Response
body: A freezer alarm triggers a door check, temperature probe reading, and backup freezer transfer if the cabinet stays above range for ten minutes. This response protects stored samples but does not replace cold chain intake scanning.

title: Slide Imaging Setup
body: Slides are cleaned, focused, and color balanced before microscope imaging. The imaging station records stain type and magnification, while storage labels and freezer checks are handled in separate workflows.

title: Drip Emitter Calibration
body: Drip irrigation emitters are calibrated by placing catch cups under three emitters per bench, running water for five minutes, weighing the collected water, and adjusting the pressure regulator until flow is even.

title: Irrigation Pressure Alarm
body: A pressure alarm opens when the irrigation header drops during a watering run. Staff inspect filters, bypass valves, and pump status, then record the flow-evenness exception without recalibrating every drip emitter.

title: Propagation Mist Schedule
body: Seedling trays in propagation receive short mist cycles during the warmest part of the day. The mist schedule keeps cuttings humid and is not used for mature fruiting beds with drip lines.

title: Nutrient Reservoir Mix
body: The fertilizer reservoir is mixed after plain water is added. Staff measure pH and electrical conductivity, then record the nutrient batch before opening the valve to the irrigation header.

title: Moisture Sensor Cleaning
body: Soil moisture probes are wiped with distilled water, checked for mineral buildup, and recalibrated in a reference pot. A dirty probe can mimic drought even when irrigation flow is normal.

title: Shade Cloth Deployment
body: Shade cloth is pulled over the south benches when the light meter crosses the summer threshold. The shade log mentions heat stress and leaf curl, but it does not change watering recipes.

title: Brake Inspection Bay
body: Tram brake inspections measure pad thickness, hydraulic pressure, and stopping distance on the service track. A car that misses the brake threshold remains in the bay until the repair lead signs release.

title: Service Release Log
body: The service release log records who signed a tram back to passenger use after brake, door, or battery work. It references threshold failures before release is signed, but the log is not the inspection that measures brake distance.

title: Door Interlock Fault
body: A door interlock fault is cleared only after the operator verifies obstruction sensors, resets the control panel, and records a successful close signal. Repeated door faults remove the tram from passenger service.

title: Battery Swap Lockout
body: Electric service carts use lockout tags before a battery pack is removed. The charged pack is scanned, seated in the cradle, and tested under load before the cart returns to the route.

title: Platform Signal Check
body: Platform signal lamps are tested at opening and after storm alerts. Maintenance confirms red, amber, and proceed indicators from the control room and logs any lamp that flickers under load.

title: Wheel Noise Triage
body: Wheel noise reports are checked for flat spots, bearing heat, and rail debris. A rhythmic thump sends the tram to wheel profiling, while a door alarm follows a different repair path.

title: Oversize Map Scanning
body: Oversize maps are photographed on the overhead scanner using fixed lights, a color target, and tile stitching. The scan operator records white balance before files move to the image review folder.

title: Image Review Folder
body: After capture, stitched map tiles enter image review for blur checks, white balance comparison, and edge alignment. Reviewers reject files that need a new scan, but they do not operate the overhead scanner.

title: Accession Review
body: New archive boxes receive accession numbers, donor restriction notes, and a shelf range before digitization. Accession review decides whether a box can be scanned, redacted, or held for curator approval.

title: Redaction Queue
body: Restricted records enter the redaction queue before public release. Staff mask sensitive fields, record the reason code, and send the redacted image to the access copy folder.

title: Metadata Authority Names
body: Catalog names are normalized against the local authority list. The metadata editor links variant spellings to one preferred name so search facets do not split the same creator across records.

title: Preservation Housing
body: Brittle papers are moved into acid free folders with buffered sleeves. Preservation housing protects originals during handling, but it does not create digital access files or authority headings.

title: Allergen Line Clearance
body: Before an allergen menu change, the prep line is cleared, utensils are sanitized, and colored tags are replaced. The lead signs the clearance sheet before the next batch starts.

title: Station Tag Audit
body: At close, the station tag audit counts colored prep tags and notes missing replacements for the next shift. It mentions allergen menu changes, but it does not authorize the changeover or sanitize utensils.

title: Cold Hold Temperature Log
body: Cold holding pans are checked every two hours. Staff record the food name, pan temperature, and corrective action if a reading rises above the safe holding limit.

title: Delivery Check In
body: Produce deliveries are checked at the receiving door for crushed packaging, short counts, and warm crates. Managers compare warm-crate rejections with two-hour pan reading trends, but delivery check-in is not the corrective action log.

title: Dish Machine Sanitizer Test
body: The dish machine sanitizer level is tested with strips at shift start and after chemical changes. A failed strip means dishes are held until the dispenser is primed and the test passes.

title: Preorder Packing Labels
body: Preorder bags are packed from the printed ticket, sealed, and labeled with pickup time. Packing staff check modifiers and side items, but allergen clearance happens before cooking begins.
